title: Radio group vs select
summary: Use radio groups when users must choose one option from a short meaningful list. Use select when the list is longer or the choice is secondary enough to collapse.
decisionRules: Prefer radio groups when a short mutually exclusive set should stay visible for comparison before the user commits.
Prefer select when the known list is roughly seven to 15 options, familiar, or secondary enough that one extra open-and-scan step is acceptable.
Do not use select to hide a critical short decision just to save vertical space; the extra click makes comparison and error recovery worse.
failureModes: Making users open a select repeatedly to compare a short list.
Rendering custom selects without native or APG-equivalent keyboard behavior.

sources.note: GOV.UK radios guidance documents visible one-of-many choices, fieldset/legend grouping, and no careless pre-selection.
USWDS radio guidance distinguishes mutually exclusive visible choices from alternatives such as checkboxes and select controls.
APG radio group guidance defines expected radio keyboard interaction, roles, labels, and checked state.
GOV.UK select guidance treats select as a compact control for longer lists, with labels, hints, errors, and caution around preselection.
USWDS select guidance recommends select sparingly for about seven to 15 options and points to radios for fewer choices.
